Notes: Confirmed Telecom demo and Haier have different licensed module sets, each correctly reflected in their own /modules response.

Test Step: Go to Settings → Modules (as tenant admin)
Expected Result: Only shows modules that Super Admin licensed
Notes: Settings -> Modules screen correctly shows only super-admin-licensed modules, with accurate licensed/enabled flags per module.

Test Step: Toggle OFF a licensed module (e.g. Activities)
Expected Result: Module disappears from sidebar for all users in this tenant
Notes: Toggling a licensed module (Voice Bot) OFF via Settings -> Modules correctly removed it from the sidebar/nav immediately.

Test Step: Toggle the module back ON
Expected Result: Module reappears in sidebar
Notes: SIGNIFICANT BUG found and fully root-caused: toggling the module back ON does NOT restore it to the sidebar. Traced to a real naming mismatch in the codebase -- the Voice Bot feature is registered internally as 'voice', but the newer module-licensing system (Settings screen, Super Admin licensing) refers to it as 'voice_bot'. The two never match, so the licensing side can say it's fully on while the actual navigation-building code can never find it. Confirmed via direct database check and a full server restart (ruling out any caching explanation). Logged as HIGH PRIORITY in Bug Report -- restored Haier's access via a super-admin workaround this session, but the underlying re-enable path remains broken for any tenant admin.
